title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony Kidd meets with Chief Inspector Bartle, Commissioner Mallow, and George Roxburgh to explain his presence at the Earl of Carrington's estate. Anthony admits that he broke into the estate at approximately 11:40 p.m. the previous night because he heard gunshots. He explains that he came to clear his name after realizing his boots had been taken by a policeman, making him a suspect due to footprints found at the scene.

Anthony provides a detailed backstory, claiming he traveled from Bulawayo to England under the alias "Jimmy McGregor" to perform a favor for a friend. He states he checked into the Britz Hotel using this alias to deliver a manuscript to a publisher. Anthony claims he received a letter from Mr. Roxburgh regarding the manuscript and an invitation to a banquet at the Earl's residence. Although he asserts he had declined the invitation, he decided to visit the estate late at night due to concerns regarding "Chimney House" and a man named Giuseppe. He describes hearing a gunshot while approaching the dark house, which prompted him to return to an inn for the night.

During the questioning, George Roxburgh becomes visibly uncomfortable when Anthony mentions the name Jimmy McGregor. Roxburgh reveals that the document in question was a letter containing the memoirs of the late Count Stapleton. Finally, Roxburgh reluctantly discloses that the man shot at the estate, who had been disguised as Count Stanislaus, was actually Prince Michael of Hesse. Chief Inspector Bartle observes Anthony's testimony with apparent satisfaction.
